Okay, so I want to talk about performance issues with your team. Okay, the biggest thing I see as people's businesses are growing is that they identify performance issues, but I don't feel, and I know this by coaching hundreds of people on it, that they ask themselves these questions first. And so what I need you to develop as an automatic reaction to any performance issue that someone's experiencing is you think about what they're doing wrong, and then automatically you think to yourself, how did I create this? Okay, and that's the only way you're going to build and grow a great company as well as help coach people. So ask yourself these questions. One, have you expressed the issue at hand? Do they know exactly how you feel? And I don't mean the like, I'm beating around the bush version. I mean like the blunt truth like, hey, you suck at this. You don't have to say it like that, but you get my point. Okay, you need to be truthful with people. Your job is to tell the truth. And so have you addressed the issue? Have you told them the truth? First thing, second thing, have you told them exactly how to fix it? If there's an issue, have you said, hey, you did this next time? This is how I would like you to handle it. Blank, blank, blank, blank. Okay, and give them step by step exactly how they should do it. Right? Three, have you provided them with support? So if somebody's struggling and they've had issues, how much extra coaching time have you got? How much extra coaching time have you given? Have you met with them for an extra one-on-one? Have you put a coaching plan to place? Have you had other peers work with them? You know, what have you done to help this person become better at whatever the issue at hand is? Right? What you need to understand is that as a leader, you are responsible for your teammate's performance. Okay? So typically the issue is either going to stem from you are the wrong leader, meaning you're not seasoned enough to have the perspective to understand how you affect everything and create all results in your business, or you have the wrong team, which typically stems from you are still not the right leader in that you keep people on your team who shouldn't be on your team. So it all comes back to you, but it's just a matter of is it you in that you have not led and been clear and coached a person enough, or is it you in that you picked the wrong person or have allowed the wrong person on your team for too long? Okay? And this is where most performance issues stem from. And so if you have expressed all of your expectations, you've given a clear path of improvement, and yet no change has occurred, this is when you need written documentation. Okay? So that brings us to Rhypes.
